title: Activity
description: One user-facing activity event. Wraps `spatie/laravel-activitylog`'s vendor `activity_log` table with two added columns (`tenant_id NOT NULL` + `application_id NULLABLE`) and six composite indexes. Polymorphic on TWO axes — `subject_type` + `subject_id` name the thing the event happened TO; `causer_type` + `causer_id` name the actor who did it. The `description` column is a human-readable narrative string authored by the developer via the model's `getActivitylogOptions()` — never a raw payload. `properties` is a JSONB blob holding event-specific context (booking_ref, facility_name, previous_values). `batch_uuid` groups rows written from one HTTP request. The `id` stays bigint auto-increment (vendor default); the `act_` ULID prefix is reserved for a future ULID conversion (Wave 7+).

x-vendor-adaptation.note: The vendor migration MUST run first — the vendor's service provider auto-registers it. Our `Schema::table` migration overlays the two columns + six indexes. Backfill from `properties.tenant_id` on existing rows via BackfillActivityTenantIdFromJsonbJob.

x-eloquent.keyPrefixNote: The `act_` prefix is reserved for a future ULID conversion (Wave 7+). Wave 6 vendor-adaptation keeps the bigint auto-increment id — the vendor's Activity model expects it, and adding a ULID prefix here would require rewriting the whole vendor read path.

id.description: Vendor bigint auto-increment. The `act_` ULID prefix is reserved (see x-eloquent.keyPrefixNote).

log_name.description: Registered feed name — 'default', 'bookings', 'auth', 'branch-management', etc. Validated by the `valid_log_name` rule against `data/log-names-catalog.json`.

description.description: Human-readable narrative authored by the developer via the model's `getActivitylogOptions()->setDescriptionForEvent()`. Truncated to 500 chars in the observer (defensive; the vendor allows longer).

subject_type.description: Polymorphic subject class (vendor morph key). Nullable because system-emitted activities may have no subject.

subject_id.description: Polymorphic subject id. May be a bigint (legacy models) or ULID/UUID string; the column is a string for cross-type compatibility.

causer_type.description: Polymorphic causer class — User / PlatformUser / ServiceAccount. Nullable for system-triggered activities.

causer_id.description: Polymorphic causer id. String for cross-type compatibility (User is UUID/ULID; PlatformUser is bigint).

event.description: Event name emitted by the trait — 'created' / 'updated' / 'deleted' / custom domain events like 'booking.confirmed'. Optional per vendor; we recommend always populating it.

properties.description: JSONB blob of event-specific context — booking_ref, facility_name, previous_values, next_values. Read by the frontend to render structured cells (chips, links) inside the description. Historical rows may carry `tenant_id` inside this blob — the backfill job reads it out into the new column.

batch_uuid.description: Groups rows written from one HTTP request. Populated by ActivityBatcher.beginBatch() at request start; cleared at request end. The feed collapses same-batch rows into 'Alice made 3 changes to Booking X'.

tenant_id.description: Owning tenant. NOT NULL for tenant-audience rows; migrated from `properties.tenant_id` for existing rows via BackfillActivityTenantIdFromJsonbJob. Applied via BelongsToTenant — every read auto-scopes.

application_id.description: Application under which the mutation happened. Auto-filled from `X-Application-Id` request header on tenant-audience writes. NULL for platform-audience rows (a PlatformUser mutation with no active application context). Enables per-application feed splits for multi-app tenants.

created_at.description: Row insert time. Sorted DESC for every feed read — the primary hot-path ordering.

updated_at.description: Vendor allows update for status transitions (e.g. flipping a `pending → confirmed` marker on an ongoing activity). Rare in practice.

indexes.name: activity_log_tenant_created_at_index
indexes.description: The primary user feed read path — 'give me the most recent activity in this tenant'. Covers `/api/v1/activities` list + WHERE tenant_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC.

indexes.name: activity_log_tenant_log_created_at_index
indexes.description: Filter by feed name — 'give me the bookings feed for this tenant'. Powers filter[log_name]=bookings.

indexes.name: activity_log_subject_created_at_index
indexes.description: Per-entity activity stream — 'what happened on this booking'. Powers /api/v1/activities/by-entity/booking/123.

indexes.name: activity_log_causer_created_at_index
indexes.description: Per-actor 'my activity' — 'what did I do this week'. Powers /api/v1/activities/mine + /api/v1/activities/by-user/{user}.

indexes.name: activity_log_batch_uuid_partial_index
indexes.description: Partial index — every batched activity in a request. Powers collapsed feed rendering + ReconcileActivityBatchesJob's orphan sweep.

indexes.name: activity_log_application_tenant_created_at_index
indexes.description: Cross-app rollup — 'give me every activity across every tenant in this Application'. Powers platform-admin cross-tenant search + per-Application feed splits.

description.description: Human-readable narrative — 'Alice booked Court 3 for tomorrow'. Truncated to 500 chars by ActivityObserver.

properties.description: Event-specific structured context. Consumers read this on the frontend to render chips + links inside the description.

batch_uuid.description: Groups activities from one HTTP request. NULL for out-of-band writes (background jobs).

application_id.description: Prefixed ULID. NULL for platform-audience rows.

human_time.description: Computed. Localised relative time — '3 minutes ago'.
